query: does a prior preterm birth increase the risk of recurrent preterm birth

retrieved_docs.title: Twin Births (Archived) -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Preventing preterm birth, one of the principal complications of twins, needs to be a clinical focus. Unlike women with single pregnancies at risk for preterm birth based on prior obstetric history,  or cerclage for short or shortening cervix, neither the placement of cervical cerclage reduces the risk of preterm labor in twin pregnancies identified to be at exceptional risk based on abbreviated cervical length or prior history of preterm birth. However, vaginal progesterone may be helpful based on preliminary understanding based on limited research data. Women with twin gestation who have preterm birth before 34 weeks may be offered supplemental progesterone for subsequent single pregnancies because preterm labor etiologies unique to plural pregnancy (for example, excessive uterine distention) may not be a principal explanation and, as such, recur in future pregnancies.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Regarding periviable PPROM, neonatal survival rates are much higher when PPROM occurs after 22 weeks (58%) than when PPROM occurs before 22 weeks gestation (14% to 22%). [52] [53] In a European series of patients known as the PPROMEXIL-III cohort, 52 of 86 (60%) patients with previable PROM delivered before 24 weeks gestation, and only 23 neonates survived until discharge. Long-term data showed that 13 of these children (15% of the original 86) had normal neurodevelopment at 2 and 5 years. [47] Women with a history of PPROM have a higher risk of recurrent PPROM and preterm birth. Progesterone supplementation should be offered in future pregnancies to reduce the risk of spontaneous preterm birth. [54]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: Patients with a history of sPTB have an increased risk for recurrent sPTB, especially if their cervical length is shortened. Recent studies have shown that vaginal progesterone supplementation does not reduce the risk of recurrent sPTB unless the patient also has a shortened cervical length. [70] [71] Vaginal progesterone supplementation is not recommended in asymptomatic individuals. Intramuscular progesterone therapy is no longer recommended for the prevention of PTB. In patients without a history of a prior sPTB, early cervical length screening is not recommended. The cervix should be assessed as part of the routine anatomy scan performed at 18 to 22 weeks EGA.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify risk factors for spontaneous preterm labor and underlying etiologies that can trigger the condition. Apply the evaluation of preterm labor, including when to use fetal fibronectin testing and how to interpret the results. Differentiate the management of preterm labor based on the patient's gestational age. Communicate the role of collaboration among interprofessional team members to improve care coordination, leading to earlier recognition of preterm labor and optimal management and outcomes. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Stillbirth -- Etiology -- Obstetric and Pregnancy-Related Conditions, Including Previous Adverse Pregnancy Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: A meta-analysis of 17 studies assessed the risk of stillbirth in mothers with prior stillbirth, preterm birth (PTB), and small for gestational age (SGA) as a proxy for fetal growth restriction. The study's results found that prior PTB or SGA birth increased the risk of stillbirth in a subsequent pregnancy (adjusted OR [aOR] 2.05, 95% CI: 1.18–3.55, and aOR 1.85, 95% CI: 1.42–2.40, respectively). The risk was even greater when both factors were present, with preterm SGA births doubling the likelihood of stillbirth (aOR 3.15, 95% CI: 1.89–5.25). [22] Additionally, a history of a previous stillbirth significantly increases the risk of recurrent stillbirth (aOR 2.68, 95% CI: 2.01–3.56). [23] These findings highlight the interconnected nature of adverse birth outcomes and the need for clinical guidelines to account for the cumulative impact of prior complications.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Etiology -- Risk Factors of Premature Rupture of Membranes
retrieved_docs.content: Risk factors for PPROM include: A history of PPROM Short cervical length Second- or third-trimester vaginal bleeding Amniocentesis and other antenatal intrauterine procedures Uterine overdistension Connective tissue disorders Congenital uterine anomalies Nutritional deficiencies of copper and ascorbic acid Low body mass index Low socioeconomic status Cigarette smoking and recreational drug use [1] [5] [6] [8] [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Pregnancy Contractions -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: In 2013, the FDA approved the use of progesterone supplementation (hydroxyprogesterone caproate) during pregnancy to reduce the risk of recurrent preterm birth in women with a history of at least 1 prior spontaneous preterm delivery. [24] It is protective against recurrent preterm birth as the rate of recurrent preterm birth with its use decreases to 25% to 31% compared with 33% to 47% in placebo controls. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Cervical Insufficiency -- Treatment / Management -- Supplemental Progesterone
retrieved_docs.content: Importantly, for patients with only a history of preterm birth undergoing cervical length screening, vaginal progesterone does not appear to reduce the risk of recurrent preterm birth in these patients without a shortened cervix. [27] The typical dosing regimen is a single 90 mg daily dose of an 8% intravaginal gel or 200 mg of micronized progesterone vaginal capsules. [28] Additionally, 17-hydroxyprogesterone caproate (17-OHP) is ineffective in reducing the risk of recurrent preterm birth in those with a pertinent history. For this reason, 17-OHP is no longer recommended to prevent recurrent preterm birth. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: PROM complicates approximately 8% of term pregnancies. After PROM at term, spontaneous labor begins within 24 hours in 60% to 70% of patients and within 72 hours in over 95%. [10] In the TERMPROM study, a large prospective randomized controlled trial comparing expectant management to immediate induction of labor, the median time to delivery in the expectant management group was approximately 33 hours. [10] Preterm PROM is less common, complicating only about 1% of deliveries overall. Previable and periviable PPROM occurring before 24 weeks gestation occurs in 0.3% to 0.4% of pregnancies. [11] Black women are twice as likely than White women to experience PPROM and are more than 6 times as likely to experience recurrent PPROM. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Spontaneous PTL is unlikely to resolve once the cervix reaches at least 3 cm. However, treatment also benefits patients who are at high risk of preterm delivery, even if they have not yet reached 3 cm dilation. Most patients presenting with symptoms of sPTL will usually deliver at term. [3] Therefore, when patients present with symptoms of sPTL, the evaluation should focus on attempting to identify patients whose labor will progress and result in preterm birth (PTB). PTL can be classified as extremely preterm. Spontaneous PTL with intact membranes is responsible for 40% to 45% of all PTBs, which, in turn, are associated with substantially higher rates of neonatal morbidity and mortality compared to neonates born after 37 weeks EGA. [4] [5] [6] [7] For this reason, accurate diagnosis and management of sPTL is imperative for improving neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Prelabor rupture of membranes (PROM) occurs when the fetal membranes rupture before the onset of labor contractions. This condition can lead to spontaneous labor or, if labor does not begin, increase the risk of complications such as intraamniotic infection and placental abruption, particularly at term. When PROM occurs before 37 weeks gestation, it is classified as preterm PROM (PPROM), posing unique challenges in managing the risk of prematurity alongside potential complications for the mother and child. PROM can arise from various causes, including membrane weakening, intraamniotic pressure, infection, and other stressors—with risk factors like a history of PROM, short cervical length, and certain lifestyle factors. Management depends largely on gestational age, with delivery often recommended after 37 weeks and more conservative approaches taken for PPROM to balance fetal development with the risk of infection.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Treatment / Management -- Late preterm (34 0/7 to 36 6/7 weeks gestation)
retrieved_docs.content: Immediate delivery is indicated if the patient is GBS-positive. However, if the patient is GBS negative or unknown, expectant management may be considered until 37 weeks. Study results have shown that immediate delivery is associated with higher rates of neonatal respiratory distress (8% versus 5%) and longer stays in intensive care (4 days versus 2 days). Expectant management is associated with lower rates of cesarean birth but increased rates of intraamniotic infection and antepartum hemorrhage. [35] [36] Given these mixed results, thorough counseling, individualized care, and shared decision-making are recommended. [1] [15] Corticosteroids are recommended, but latency antibiotics and tocolytics are not. Patients who are GBS unknown should be screened for GBS with a rectovaginal swab and started on GBS prophylaxis, which can be discontinued if the culture returns negative. [24]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Approximately 30% of sPTL cases resolve spontaneously, and approximately 50% of patients hospitalized with sPTL ultimately deliver at term. Furthermore, less than 10% of individuals presenting with preterm contractions progress to active labor and sPTB within 7 days. [1] [65] Cervical length and fFN can be used to stratify patients into high- and low-risk categories.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Etiology -- Etiology of Premature Rupture of Membranes
retrieved_docs.content: Oxidative stress generates reactive oxygen species, which can target and degrade collagen. Reactive oxygen species are most commonly produced during cellular respiration and when they are released by immune cells during phagocytosis. Typically, these reactive oxygen species are balanced by antioxidants. However, the reactive oxygen species and antioxidants can become imbalanced due to dietary deficiencies of antioxidants, tobacco use, and high fetoplacental energy demands, including those seen in pregnancies at high altitudes or with concordant intrinsic microvascular disease. [2] Additionally, evidence demonstrates that some single-nucleotide polymorphisms involved in the metabolism of the extracellular matrix can significantly affect the risk of PPROM. [7] However, despite various underlying etiologies, no apparent cause is often identified in patients presenting with PROM and PPROM.

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm and Term Prelabor Rupture of Membranes (PPROM and PROM) -- Etiology -- Etiology of Premature Rupture of Membranes
retrieved_docs.content: PROM or PPROM may occur for various reasons, including normal physiologic weakening of the membranes at term, increased intraamniotic pressure, intraamniotic infection or inflammation, oxidative stress, and membrane remodeling capacity abnormalities. [1] [2] [5] Inflammation can activate proteases that degrade collagen within the fetal membranes, weakening and predisposing them to rupture. Intrauterine infection is also a common cause of inflammation. However, while intraamniotic infection is seen in a significant proportion of patients with PPROM, it may be either a cause or a result of membrane weakening. For example, while an ascending genital infection from bacterial vaginosis can certainly cause an innate inflammatory response, sterile inflammation from bleeding or tobacco use may also weaken the fetal membranes, allowing for microbial invasion. [2] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Retinopathy of Prematurity -- Pathophysiology -- Risk Amplification by Exogenous Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Prolonged oxygen supplementation and mechanical ventilation exacerbate the hyperoxic insult. Sepsis, anemia, and poor postnatal weight gain also increase susceptibility by influencing systemic inflammation and angiogenic regulation. [40]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849
retrieved_docs.content: Poor reproductive history and elective termination of pregnancy are clinically associated with preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This relationship indicates that women who have experienced a suboptimal reproductive history or who have undergone an elective termination of pregnancy may be at increased risk for developing preterm labor. Recognizing these associations can help clinicians identify patients who require closer monitoring and targeted interventions to prevent or manage preterm labor, thereby improving maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 259
retrieved_docs.content: The community describes 17‑α‑hydroxyprogesterone as having a preventive effect on spontaneous abortion (إجهاض تلقائي), which is classified as a child of pregnancy complications (مضاعفات الحمل) with subcategories missed abortion (إجهاض فائت) and recurrent abortion (إجهاض معتاد). Spontaneous abortion is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), which in turn is linked to neonatal death (وفاة حديث الولادة) and infant preterm low birth weight. Cervical incompetence/insufficiency, a non‑inflammatory disorder of the female genital tract, is placed under pelvic organ abnormalities complicating pregnancy and is associated with cerclage, high‑risk obstetric profiles, preterm low birth weight, neonatal death, preterm delivery, poor reproductive history, habitual abortion, elective termination, and overall high‑risk status. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1697
retrieved_docs.content: A caesarean section scar of unknown type and a poor reproductive history are clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor), indicating that women who have an indeterminate type of previous cesarean scar or who have experienced suboptimal reproductive outcomes are at increased risk for premature labor. Recognizing these associations enables clinicians to identify high‑risk patients, implement closer surveillance, and consider preventive strategies to reduce the likelihood of preterm delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2316
retrieved_docs.content: A poor reproductive history, reflecting previous obstetric complications or adverse outcomes, is clinically associated with an increased risk of preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This connection highlights the importance of detailed reproductive assessments in prenatal care to identify women who may be more likely to experience early delivery and to apply targeted monitoring and preventive strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1741
retrieved_docs.content: Intrauterine pregnancy is closely linked to a range of obstetric events. It is clinically associated with “other pregnancy and delivery including normal,” indicating that a normally progressing intrauterine gestation often leads to standard delivery outcomes. At the same time, intrauterine pregnancy shows clinical associations with cesarean delivery (الولادة القيصرية), endometritis (الْتِهابُ بِطانَةِ الرَّحِمِ), and the procedure described as ربط البوق, suggesting that these conditions frequently occur together in the context of intrauterine gestation. Cesarean delivery (الولادة القيصرية) itself is clinically associated with preterm birth (ولادة قبل الأوان) and with infant outcomes such as preterm low birth weight (INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT), highlighting the potential impact of surgical delivery on neonatal maturity and weight. Additionally, cesarean delivery co‑occurs with ربط البوق, reinforcing the connection between this surgical approach and the aforementioned procedure. “Other pregnancy and delivery including normal” co‑occurs with preterm birth (ولادة قبل الأوان) and also has a relationship with cesarean delivery (الولادة القيصرية), indicating that even pregnancies classified as normal can be linked to early delivery and may sometimes require surgical intervention. The condition described as حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ co‑occurs with ربط البوق, further emphasizing the recurrent association of ربط البوق with various pregnancy‑related states. Overall, the network of relationships demonstrates that intrauterine pregnancy, normal delivery, preterm birth, cesarean delivery, infant preterm low birth weight, endometritis, and the procedure ربط البوق are interwoven in clinical practice, with multiple conditions frequently occurring together or influencing one another, which is essential information for comprehensive obstetric assessment and patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 4 -- Community 2821
retrieved_docs.content: Threat of premature labor (تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر) is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), indicating that individuals who are shorter in height may have an increased risk of experiencing preterm labor. This association highlights the importance of recognizing short stature as a potential clinical indicator when assessing pregnant patients for the likelihood of premature delivery, thereby informing monitoring strategies and preventive care measures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: Infant preterm low birth weight is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), indicating that children born prematurely with low birth weight have an increased risk of developing reduced height later in life.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2855
retrieved_docs.content: A poor reproductive history (REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR) is clinically associated with preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان), and preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان) is clinically associated with pelvic inflammatory disease (مرض التهاب الحوض). This interrelationship indicates that women with an adverse reproductive background may have an elevated risk of experiencing premature labor, and that premature labor may be linked to underlying pelvic inflammatory disease, underscoring the importance of comprehensive pelvic health assessment in patients with a history of poor reproductive outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 2062
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with both الانفصال المبكر للمشيمة (early placental separation) and مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor). In addition, REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR is clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان, indicating that an unknown‑type cesarean section scar and a poor reproductive history are both risk factors for preterm labor, while the unknown‑type scar also predisposes to early placental separation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 2 -- Community 820
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature), indicating that infants born prematurely with low birth weight often exhibit reduced growth potential. Similarly, تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) is also clinically associated with قصر القامة, suggesting that women who experience threatened preterm labor may give birth to children who are at risk for short stature. In the broader obstetric context, LABOR AND PARTURITION includes مخاض مهدد (threatened labor) as a parent category, reflecting that threatened labor is a recognized sub‑type within the labor and delivery process. The classification system further defines early or threatened labor as encompassing both تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) and مخاض مهدد (threatened labor), grouping these conditions together under a common clinical umbrella. Together, these relationships underscore the importance of recognizing threatened labor states—both preterm and term—as risk factors for adverse growth outcomes such as short stature, and they highlight the need for integrated obstetric monitoring and pediatric follow‑up to address potential long‑term growth implications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1449
retrieved_docs.content: Preterm low‑birth‑weight infants are clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature). Short stature itself is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), indicating that children who remain short may have an increased risk of dying in the newborn period. Additionally, تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) is clinically associated with قصر القامة, suggesting that pregnancies complicated by early labor threats often result in offspring who develop short stature. Collectively, these relationships highlight short stature as a common clinical link among preterm low birth weight, threatened preterm labor, and adverse neonatal outcomes such as neonatal death, underscoring its relevance for risk assessment and early intervention in perinatal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2825
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature), indicating that infants born preterm with low birth weight often experience growth restriction that results in reduced adult height. In turn, قصر القامة is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), suggesting that short stature in this context reflects severe underlying health challenges that increase the risk of death shortly after birth. Recognizing the link between low‑birth‑weight preterm infants, subsequent short stature, and heightened neonatal mortality can guide clinicians in early monitoring and intervention strategies to improve outcomes for this vulnerable population.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 2360
retrieved_docs.content: Medical databases are clinically associated with several obstetric conditions, including تمزق الأغشية الباكر (premature rupture of membranes), نواتج متبقية من الحمل (residual pregnancy outcomes), and gestation twin (twin gestation). These associations indicate that such databases contain relevant clinical data on the incidence, risk factors, and management of these conditions. Additionally, the low transverse cesarean section technique is clinically associated with تمزق الأغشية الباكر, suggesting a link between this common surgical approach and the occurrence, diagnosis, or treatment of premature rupture of membranes. Recognizing these relationships helps clinicians leverage database information to improve decision‑making and patient care in obstetric practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 2 -- Community 1094
retrieved_docs.content: Prematurity (خديج) is closely linked to several obstetric and fetal conditions. It is clinically associated with cervical insufficiency (قصور عنق الرحم), can arise as a consequence of pulmonary hypoplasia syndrome (متلازمة خلل نضج الرئة), and is related to the occurrence of preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). Cervical insufficiency itself is recognized in both the CDRH Health Effect clinical terminology and the FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health terminology as a distinct concept. Management of cervical insufficiency often involves the cerclage procedure, and the condition is frequently observed in patients with a poor reproductive history, recurrent miscarriage (إجهاض معتاد), and those who have undergone elective termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري). Preterm labor is similarly included in the CDRH and FDA terminologies, is associated with an unknown type of caesarean section scar (CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN), and is linked to poor reproductive history and elective pregnancy termination. Additionally, the FDA terminology categorizes missed abortion (إجهاض فائت) and infertility (العقم) within its related subsets, underscoring the broader spectrum of reproductive complications that intersect with cervical insufficiency, prematurity, and preterm labor. Understanding these interconnections aids clinicians in identifying risk factors, guiding preventive strategies such as cerclage, and optimizing care for patients experiencing these intertwined obstetric challenges.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 1773
retrieved_docs.content: Databases are clinically associated with a range of obstetric conditions, including ولادة قبل الأوان (preterm birth), الْتِهابُ المَشيماءِ والسَّلَى (inflammation of the placenta and membranes), نواتج متبقية من الحمل (post‑pregnancy residuals), gestation twin (twin gestation), and الولادة القيصرية (cesarean delivery). The relationship between الولادة القيصرية and ولادة قبل الأوان indicates that cesarean delivery is clinically linked to preterm birth, reflecting the frequent need for surgical delivery in early‑gestation births. Moreover, the entity “Operation to facilitate delivery” is defined as a type of الولادة القيصرية, underscoring that cesarean delivery is a surgical procedure performed to assist the birthing process. Together, these connections highlight how clinical databases capture essential information on various pregnancy‑related complications and interventions, and they illustrate the interdependence of cesarean delivery with preterm birth and other obstetric conditions, which is important for patient management and outcome monitoring.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1610
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with الولادة القيصرية (Cesarean delivery) and with قصر القامة (short stature). In addition, تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) is clinically associated with قصر القامة. These relationships indicate that short stature is linked both to infants born preterm with low birth weight and to the condition of threatened preterm labor, while preterm low‑birth‑weight infants are more likely to be delivered by Cesarean section. Recognizing these interconnections can help clinicians anticipate delivery method needs and monitor growth outcomes in infants at risk for or experiencing preterm birth complications.
